name: Vietnamese Crab Spring Rolls
recipeIngredient: 2 oz Dried bean thread noodles (Mung bean)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the mung bean noodles and tree-ear mushrooms in warm water until soft and pliable. Drain the noodles and cut into shorter lengths; set aside.
2. Remove the hard centers from the mushrooms, coarsely chop, and set aside.
3. In a medium bowl, combine the pork, garlic, fish sauce, and ground pepper. Mix in crab meat, tree-ear mushrooms, carrots, bean sprouts, shallots, and bean thread noodles; set aside.
4. Combine warm water and sugar in a bowl.
5. Lay 1 rice paper wrapper on a damp towel (keep unused wrappers in their package). Generously brush the wrapper with the sugar water to allow it to soften for about 30 seconds or longer, depending on the brand.
6. Shape 3 tablespoons of filling into a tight compact cylinder that is 1-inch-wide by 4-to-5 inches long.
7. Place the cylinder along the lower edge of the wrapper and roll up tightly. Fold both outside edges inward to enclose the ends.
8. Continue rolling up into a cylinder; set aside covered with plastic wrap. Repeat until all rolls are done. Ensure the roll is firm and evenly packed, or bandage any tears with softened rice-paper remnants.
9. Heat peanut oil in a preheated wok or saucepan to 325°F. Add a few rolls at a time without crowding.
10. Increase heat to 375°F and fry for 10 seconds. Fry the rolls until golden brown, about 6-8 minutes.
11. Remove from oil and drain on paper towel.
12. Cut crosswise into 1½-inch pieces.
13. Arrange rolls on a plate lined with lettuce.
14. On another plate, arrange leaves of lettuce, fresh coriander, and mint leaves.
15. To eat, wrap spring-roll garnish with sprig of coriander and mint leaves in a lettuce leaf and dip into nuoc cham sauce (See Recipe).
